MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action And For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $7,260.00; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Michael Avanesian, Esq. And Alen Petrosyan In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Advanced From Nov-19-18 To Nov-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action And For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $7,260.00; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Michael Avanesian, Esq. And Alen Petrosyan In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
**Defendant Alen Pterosyan's Demurrer to Complaint and Motion for order to Expunge notice of pendency of action and for attorney fees and costs in the amountof $7,260.00 are heard concurrently.**

Appearances:
John H. cigavic III, esq. (415-859-5065) of Basic Legal Services for and with Plaintiffs/opposing party Aurora Martinez Sangil and Santiago Sangil.
Arash Moussavian, esq. (415-695-4548) of JT Legal for Defendant/Moving party Alen Pterosyan.

Plaintiff ARGUED re: Grant Deed that started the chain was transferred to a non-existent entity; New Horizon Consulting LLC did not exist in 2014 - existed in 2016; New Horizon Consulting, Inc. was dissolved in 2015. Defendant ARGUED that his client purchased the property in 2016 after a short sale in 2014 and the Sangils have not paid anything since 2014. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ALEN PTEROSYAN MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action And For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $7,260.00 is Taken Under Submission. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Arash Moussavian, esq. (415-695-4548) of JT Legal for Defendant/Moving party Alen Pterosyan.

Plaintiff ARGUED re: Grant Deed that started the chain was transferred to a non-existent entity; New Horizon Consulting LLC did not exist in 2014 - existed in 2016; New Horizon Consulting, Inc. was dissolved in 2015. Defendant ARGUED that his client purchased the property in 2016 after a short sale in 2014 and the Sangils have not paid anything since 2014. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
